reasoning: The E2 interface is identified as the point of origin due to high packet loss and latency anomalies. Other interface anomalies (F1-c, F1-u, A1) are viewed as symptomatic propagation from O-DU and Near-RT RIC instability.
actionable_steps: 1. Verify O-DU E2 Agent: Check the health and resource utilization (CPU/Memory) of the E2 agent running on the O-DU. 2. Network Path Audit: Inspect the transport network between the O-DU and the Near-RT RIC for congestion or faulty hardware. 3. Configuration Review: Ensure that E2 interface timers and window sizes are correctly configured to handle current traffic loads.
